---
title: Embed the app
description: Render the Ready-to-Launch banking experience on web, React Native, iOS, and Android — including Content Security Policy and logout cleanup.
sidebar:
  order: 1
---

import { Tabs, TabItem, Steps } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The embed is the heart of a Ready-to-Launch integration: a single component that renders onboarding *and* the full banking app, switching automatically based on the user's state. This page covers every platform and the two things teams most often miss — **Content Security Policy** and **logout cleanup**.

## Choose your platform

<Tabs syncKey="platform">
<TabItem label="Web">

**1. Load the components bundle** in your `<head>`:

```html
<!-- Sandbox -->
<script type="module" src="https://ui.s.unit.sh/release/latest/components-extended.es.js"></script>

<!-- Production -->
<script type="module" src="https://ui.unit.co/release/latest/components-extended.es.js"></script>
```

**2. Render the element**, passing the JWT you minted for the signed-in user:

```html
<unit-elements-white-label-app jwt-token="{{JwtToken}}"></unit-elements-white-label-app>
```

**Or create it dynamically:**

```js
const app = document.createElement('unit-elements-white-label-app');
app.setAttribute('jwt-token', jwtToken);
document.querySelector('#unit-app-placeholder').append(app);
```

The component is a standard custom element rendered in a shadow DOM, so your page styles won't leak in and the component's styles won't leak out. Brand it through [theming](/build/theming/), not page CSS.

</TabItem>
<TabItem label="React / React Native">

Unit ships SDKs for React Native (and the web component works directly in React — just render the custom element and pass `jwt-token`).

```jsx
// React (web) — the custom element works as-is.
export function Banking({ jwtToken }) {
  return <unit-elements-white-label-app jwt-token={jwtToken} />;
}
```

For **React Native**, initialize the SDK and mount the provided component, authenticating with the JWT (or customer token). The SDK exposes a cleanup method to clear user data on logout — call it instead of touching `localStorage` directly.

:::note
Native SDK package names, init signatures, and cleanup method names are versioned. Confirm the current API for your SDK version with your solution engineer or the SDK README — the shape (init → mount → cleanup-on-logout) is stable even as names change.
:::

</TabItem>
<TabItem label="iOS (Swift)">

Add the Unit iOS SDK, initialize it, and instantiate the white-label component with the user's JWT (or customer token). On logout, call the SDK's dedicated cleanup method to clear stored user data.

```swift
// Shape (confirm exact names against your SDK version):
// 1. UnitSDK.initialize(environment: .sandbox)
// 2. let view = UnitWhiteLabelApp(jwtToken: jwtToken)
// 3. UnitSDK.clearUserData()   // on logout
```

</TabItem>
<TabItem label="Android (Kotlin)">

Add the Unit Android SDK, initialize it, and instantiate the white-label component with the user's JWT (or customer token). On logout, call the SDK's dedicated cleanup method.

```kotlin
// Shape (confirm exact names against your SDK version):
// 1. UnitSdk.initialize(context, Environment.SANDBOX)
// 2. val view = UnitWhiteLabelApp(context, jwtToken = jwtToken)
// 3. UnitSdk.clearUserData()   // on logout
```

</TabItem>
</Tabs>

## Content Security Policy (web)

The embed loads several third parties at runtime — identity verification vendors, Plaid, and Zendesk for support chat. If your site sends a `Content-Security-Policy`, you must allow them or the app will silently fail to load parts of the flow.

```html title="Add to your page (or send as a CSP header)"
<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="
  connect-src 'self' https://*.s.unit.sh https://*.unit.co https://*.zdassets.com
    https://*.zendesk.com https://cdn.plaid.com https://*.verygoodvault.com
    https://*.vouched.id https://*.au10tixservices.com;
  script-src 'self' https://*.zdassets.com https://*.zendesk.com https://cdn.plaid.com
    https://js.verygoodvault.com https://*.vouched.id https://*.au10tixservices.com;
  frame-src 'self' https://*.zendesk.com https://cdn.plaid.com https://*.verygoodvault.com
    https://*.vouched.id https://*.au10tixservices.com;
"/>
```

:::tip
For production, prefer sending CSP as an **HTTP response header** rather than a `<meta>` tag, and fold these allowances into your existing policy rather than replacing it. Swap the sandbox hosts (`*.s.unit.sh`) appropriately — `*.unit.co` covers production.
:::

## Logout cleanup (web)

The web embed stores the end-user's banking session in `localStorage`. **You must clear it** when the user logs out of your app, and you should also clear it after 24 hours of inactivity:

```js title="On logout"
localStorage.removeItem('unitCustomerToken');
localStorage.removeItem('unitVerifiedCustomerToken');
```

Clearing it keeps sessions clean across users:

- **Session bleed** — without cleanup, the next user on a shared device can land in the previous user's banking session.
- **Stale state** — leftover tokens can make the embed behave inconsistently; clearing them resolves it.

:::note
Make logout cleanup part of your standard sign-out flow, and see the [Security model](/launch/security/) for the full guidance. On native, use the SDK's cleanup method instead of clearing storage yourself.
:::

## What the component renders, and when

You render the **same** element for every signed-in user. The component decides what to show based on the user's state behind the JWT:

- **No application yet** → onboarding (the application form).
- **Application in progress / pending** → status screens.
- **Approved** → the full banking app (accounts, cards, payments).

If you only want the onboarding portion (and to run your own UI afterward), use the [embedded application form](/build/embedded-application-form/) instead.

## Next step
